examples.title: Basic System Call Example - File Operations
examples.description: Demonstrates open, read, write system calls with error handling
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates three fundamental Unix system calls: open() to get a file descriptor, read() to read data, and close() to release the descriptor. It shows proper error handling using errno and perror(). The file descriptor returned by open() is used as an index in the process's file descriptor table.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for read where n is bytes read

examples.title: Process Creation with fork()
examples.description: Demonstrates process creation using fork system call
examples.explanation: The fork() system call creates a new process by duplicating the parent. In the parent, it returns the child's PID; in the child, it returns 0. The parent uses waitpid() to wait for child termination and retrieve exit status. This demonstrates process control system calls.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for fork, O(n) where n is process lifespan for wait

examples.title: Parent-Child Communication via Pipe
examples.description: Demonstrates IPC using pipe system call for inter-process communication
examples.explanation: The pipe() system call creates a unidirectional communication channel. pipefd[0] is the read end, pipefd[1] is the write end. After fork(), the parent closes its read end and writes, while child closes write end and reads. This demonstrates classic Unix IPC and proper file descriptor management in multi-process programs.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is message size for write/read
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for pipe buffer and child buffer
